mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce discount rates applied to equity cash flows, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show negative mean or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows when FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.5 percent signals labor market deterioration that triggers immediate risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and aggregate retail sales confirms broad-based demand growth, driving corporate earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to produce positive 60-day forward returns in > 55% of observations, or interaction term Rank IC < 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A declining industrial production index during a late-cycle expansion indicates supply-side bottlenecks and margin compression, forcing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases yield positive 20-day forward returns in > 60% of cases, or regime-specific Rank IC remains below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rates above 2.0 percent impose financing costs that suppress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in > 70% of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with a stable or declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor demand, supporting earnings growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of PAYEMS growth and UNRATE stability yields negative 20-day forward returns in > 58% of observations, or interaction Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing housing starts reflect strong consumer confidence and construction sector activity, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST increases fail to predict positive 60-day forward returns, with mean return <= 0 or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: An industrial production index above 108 signals robust manufacturing output that supports corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates negative 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where INDPRO exceeds 108.
